q2_chain_seeds: PH-02 (file:// pull) chains into PH-06 (ENTRYPOINT RCE) and PH-16 (time-bomb)
PH-11 (registry.Local simple-request pull) chains into PH-13 (MCP RCE)
PH-14 (blob injection) chains into GGUF parser CVEs (DoS/crash)
PH-CX2 (preflight via Fallback) chains into PH-CX1 (registry.Local independent of CORS fix)
q3_fragile_items: PH-05: null origin behavior — needs runtime testing of gin-contrib/cors v1.7.2
q5_impact_multipliers: PH-16 (ConfigV2 time-bomb): if validated fully with poc config, changes severity of ALL existing pull operations to CRITICAL
PH-CX5 (vscode-webview): if VS Code extension supply-chain attack confirmed, widens browser-based attack to users without file:// null-origin issue
loops.reasoning: Q1: All entry points covered (gin routes, registry.Local, Entrypoint/MCP, blob upload). Q3: Only PH-05 is fragile — null origin behavior — but the attack is fully valid via other CORS-allowed origins (vscode-webview://, app://, tauri://), so PH-05's ambiguity does not block overall findings. Q4: No untouched areas. All trust chain gaps addressed. Max coverage achieved in loop 1.
